title: Buttery Chewy Norwegian Haddock Soup
description: A mild, comforting soup featuring tender Norwegian haddock in a buttery, lightly seasoned broth—ideal for a sick day and kosher-friendly.
instructions: Melt the butter in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the diced onion, carrot, and celery; sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Pour in the fish stock and water, then stir in the bay leaf, dill, and diced potato.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.
Add the haddock fillets and continue to simmer gently for another 6-8 minutes, until the fish flakes easily with a fork.
Season with salt, pepper, and a splash of lemon juice; remove the bay leaf.
Serve the soup hot, garnished with additional dill if desired.
